calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Sep-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
GABRIEL LEE-SANCHEZ, Esq., of KEESAL, YOUNG & LOGAN APC., Tel: (415) 398-6000 for petitioner
Shannon Marquardt self- represented respondent.
Roberth Loventhal special appearing for respondent - observinbg.

Hearing was held. A timely Notice of Contest was received from Petitioner's counsel. Contest the portion of the tentative ruling continuing the hearing on November 14, 2025. Cousnel state reasons why this motion should not be continue 1. Procedurally improper because CCP 1005(b) requires moving papers to be served and filed at least 16 court days before the hearing. Here, the Motion was served five (5) court days after the hearing date. Ms. Marquardt unilaterally selected and merely two (2) days before the hearing on Mr. Jackson's Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award. 2. Granting a continuance would be a waste of judicial resources because Ms. Marquardt's mandatory statutory deadlines to seek to vacate the Arbitration Award and to oppose Mr. Jackson's Petition have passed. The matter argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, the Court vacates its tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER DEAN JACKSON'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is Granted. Counsel for Petitioner required to prepare a proposed order confirming the arbitration award and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: John M. True; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMT)

Renewed Motion To Continue Hearing Scheduled For September 12, 2025
Off Calendar Sep-12-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
GABRIEL LEE-SANCHEZ, Esq., of KEESAL, YOUNG & LOGAN APC., Tel: (415) 398-6000 for petitioner
Shannon Marquardt self- represented respondent.
Roberth Loventhal special appearing for respondent - observinbg.

Hearing was held. A timely Notice of Contest was received from Petitioner's counsel. Contest the portion of the tentative ruling continuing the hearing on November 14, 2025. Cousnel state reasons why this motion should not be continue 1. Procedurally improper because CCP 1005(b) requires moving papers to be served and filed at least 16 court days before the hearing. Here, the Motion was served five (5) court days after the hearing date. Ms. Marquardt unilaterally selected and merely two (2) days before the hearing on Mr. Jackson's Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award. 2. Granting a continuance would be a waste of judicial resources because Ms. Marquardt's mandatory statutory deadlines to seek to vacate the Arbitration Award and to oppose Mr. Jackson's Petition have passed. The matter argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, the Court vacates its tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT SHANNON MARQUARDT'S Renewed Motion To Continue Hearing Scheduled For September 12, 2025 is DENIED.

Judge: John M. True; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMT)
